preregistered_question: does criticality alone confound M_diss (Sec 14.1), like it confounds gating (Exp I)?
verdict: GROWING CONFOUND NEAR TRUE CRITICALITY (nuanced, not a clean survival): the reproduction fraction at the D>=50% threshold is only 25% (3/12), but D_bare_critical rises MONOTONICALLY with criticality and low noise -- from 0.10 at sigma=0.70 to 0.66 at sigma=0.97 (low-noise column) -- reaching 73% of the identity-linked reference (0.90) at the strongest/cleanest cell. The sub-critical control stays low (D<=0.10), confirming it is criticality specifically, not the statistic, that drives this. The honest reading: M_diss is NOT confounded away from true criticality, but AS a system approaches sigma=1 with low read-out noise, the dissociation signature converges toward what pure criticality alone can produce -- mirroring exactly the pattern Exp I found for gating (safe away from criticality, confounded near it). Sec 14.1 should state this boundary explicitly: dissociation evidence is diagnostic of identity only if the boundary process can be shown to be measurably sub-critical, which CBRA does not currently require.
